Question: An airplane leaves Chicago and makes the 3000-km trip to Los Angeles in 5.0 h. A second plane leaves Chicago one-half hour later and arrives in Los Angeles at the same time. Compare the average velocities of the two planes. Ignore the curvature of Earth and the difference in altitude between the two cities.
Answer: Take west to be the positive direction. 1st plane: $$\overset{-}{\nu} = 600 \text{km}/\text{h}$$ 2nd plane $$\overset{-}{\nu} = 667.0 \text{km}/\text{h}$$
o3 Answer:
Flight 1  
Distance: 3000 km  
Time: 5.0 h  
Average velocity  
v₁ = 3000 km / 5.0 h = 600 km·h⁻¹ (≈ 1.67 × 10² m·s⁻¹)

Flight 2  
Leaves 0.5 h later but lands at the same moment; therefore its flight time is  
5.0 h – 0.5 h = 4.5 h.  
Average velocity  
v₂ = 3000 km / 4.5 h ≈ 6.67 × 10² km·h⁻¹ (≈ 1.85 × 10² m·s⁻¹)

Comparison  
v₂ / v₁ = (3000/4.5) / (3000/5.0) = 5.0 / 4.5 ≈ 1.11.

Thus the second plane’s average speed is about 6.7 × 10² km/h, roughly 11 % faster than the first plane’s 600 km/h.

Question: An object has an acceleration of $$+ 1.2 \text{cm/s}^{2}$$ . At $$t = 4.0 \text{s}$$ , its velocity is $$- 3.4 \text{cm/s}$$ . Determine the object’s velocities at $$t = 1.0 \text{s}$$ and $$t = 6.0 \text{s}$$ .
o3 Answer:
Because the acceleration is uniform, the velocity changes linearly with time:

v = v₀ + a Δt ,

where  

• a = +1.2 cm s⁻² (constant)  
• v₀ is the velocity at the reference time t₀  
• Δt = t − t₀.

The reference data given is
t₀ = 4.0 s , v₀ = −3.4 cm s⁻¹.

1. Velocity at t = 1.0 s  

Δt = 1.0 s − 4.0 s = −3.0 s  

v(1 s) = −3.4 cm s⁻¹ + (1.2 cm s⁻²)(−3.0 s)  
    = −3.4 cm s⁻¹ − 3.6 cm s⁻¹  
    = −7.0 cm s⁻¹.

2. Velocity at t = 6.0 s  

Δt = 6.0 s − 4.0 s = +2.0 s  

v(6 s) = −3.4 cm s⁻¹ + (1.2 cm s⁻²)(2.0 s)  
    = −3.4 cm s⁻¹ + 2.4 cm s⁻¹  
    = −1.0 cm s⁻¹.

Therefore  

v(1.0 s) = −7.0 cm s⁻¹  
v(6.0 s) = −1.0 cm s⁻¹.

Question: A particle moving at constant acceleration has velocities of $$2.0 \text{m/s}$$ at $$t = 2.0$$ s and $$- 7.6 \text{m/s}$$ at $$t = 5.2$$ s. What is the acceleration of the particle?
o3 Answer:
For motion with constant acceleration, the acceleration a is given by the change in velocity divided by the change in time:

a = (v₂ − v₁)/(t₂ − t₁)

Given  
v₁ = 2.0 m s⁻¹ at t₁ = 2.0 s  
v₂ = −7.6 m s⁻¹ at t₂ = 5.2 s

Δv = v₂ − v₁ = (−7.6 m s⁻¹) − (2.0 m s⁻¹) = −9.6 m s⁻¹  
Δt = t₂ − t₁ = 5.2 s − 2.0 s = 3.2 s  

a = Δv / Δt = (−9.6 m s⁻¹) / (3.2 s) = −3.0 m s⁻²

Acceleration of the particle: −3.0 m s⁻² (the negative sign indicates it is directed opposite to the positive velocity direction at t = 2.0 s).

(a) What is the electron’s velocity when it emerges from this region? b) How long does the electron take to cross the region?

v² = u² + 2as

u² = (4.0 × 10⁵)² = 1.6 × 10¹¹

v² = 1.6 × 10¹¹ + 6.0 × 10¹¹ = 7.6 × 10¹¹  
v = √(7.6 × 10¹¹) ≈ 8.7 × 10⁵ m s⁻¹

Distance in last second = $75.0 \text{ m} - 41.55 \text{ m} = 33.45 \text{ m}$
